channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Enriched membrane stackings made possible
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 12 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02530-9

A sacrificial layer with hexagonal symmetry is reported that can be used to peel and delaminate oxide substrates with hexagonal symmetry and beyond.
item.dc:title: Enriched membrane stackings made possible
item.dc:creator: Atsushi Tsukazaki

item.title: Unravel the complexity
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 10 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02536-3

Fitting the resistance fluctuation data with a hidden Markov model helps uncover the complex free energy landscape and understand the glass relaxation dynamics of a phase-change material, GeTe.
item.dc:title: Unravel the complexity
item.dc:creator: Jiang-Jing Wang
Wei Zhang

item.title: Two-dimensional crystalline hard masks for high-aspect-ratio nanofabrication
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 10 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02524-7

Van der Waals metal oxyhalides demonstrate high resistance to aggressive plasma etching, precise patternability and mechanical transferability, enabling high-aspect-ratio nanofabrication across diverse substrates.
item.dc:title: Two-dimensional crystalline hard masks for high-aspect-ratio nanofabrication
item.dc:creator: Pranavram Venkatram
Ziheng Chen
Krishnendu Mukhopadhyay
Bob Hengstebeck
Lei Ding
Vlastimil Mazanek
Yang Yang
Zdenek Sofer
Saptarshi Das

item.title: Room-temperature two-dimensional multiferroic metal with voltage-controllable magnetic order
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 09 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02537-2

Bilayer CrTe2 films grown via molecular beam epitaxy are found to host room-temperature multiferroicity and strong magnetoelectric coupling.
item.dc:title: Room-temperature two-dimensional multiferroic metal with voltage-controllable magnetic order
item.dc:creator: Dacheng Tian
Shulin Zhong
Jianyu Dong
Song Zhou
Zhiwen Liu
Kai Chen

Peng Cheng

Baojie Feng
Kehui Wu

Yi Du
Yunhao Lu
Lan Chen

item.title: An iontronic reservoir for highly robust neuromorphic prosthesis
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 09 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02532-7

Achieving robustness against a dynamic physiological environment is a challenge for neuromorphic prostheses. An interface made of a self-healing hydrogel and electrodes acts as a physical reservoir of a neuromorphic prosthesis, achieving minimal susceptibility to physical damage.
item.dc:title: An iontronic reservoir for highly robust neuromorphic prosthesis
item.dc:creator: Mengjiao Pei
Tian Gao
Li Liu
Wenlong Li
Haotian Long
Yifei Luo

Xiaodong Chen
Changjin Wan

item.title: Proton shuttle-assisted triplet energy transfer
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 09 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02535-4

Proton-coupled energy transfer has only been observed with singlet-exciton transfer. A net process of spin-triplet migration is observed in photoexcited colloidal quantum dots with surface-anchored molecular moieties, establishing a proton shuttle-assisted triplet energy transfer.
item.dc:title: Proton shuttle-assisted triplet energy transfer
item.dc:creator: Zhaolong Wang
Jingyi Zhu
Kaifeng Wu
